projectDescription: A command-line tool that automatically extracts and deobfuscates strings from malware binaries using advanced static analysis and emulation techniques.
keyFeatures: Automatic extraction of static ASCII and UTF-16LE strings
Stack string extraction via emulation of runtime string construction
Tight string extraction for strings decoded on the stack
Decoded string extraction by identifying and emulating decoding routines
Language-specific string extraction for Go and Rust binaries
JSON output for integration with other tools and scripts
Integration scripts for IDA Pro, Ghidra, Binary Ninja, Radare2, and x64dbg
publicApiSurface: floss <sample> — analyze a binary and extract all string types
floss --only {static,stack,tight,decoded} — enable only specific string types
floss -l/--load — load from existing FLOSS results JSON
floss --language {go,rust,none} — select or disable language-specific extraction
floss -v/--verbose — enable verbose output with function offsets
floss -q/--quiet — suppress formatting, print one string per line
floss --signatures <path> — provide custom FLIRT signature files
floss -L/--large-file — allow processing files larger than default size limit
ResultDocument — Pydantic dataclass representing FLOSS analysis output
StaticString, StackString, TightString, DecodedString — string result types
